stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 39% and 52%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $182K in good weather patterns, $66K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $297K in good weather patterns, $11K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $256K in good weather patterns, $149K in bad weather patterns. corn: $178K in good weather patterns, $24K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.46: best option is soybeans with EV=$198.2K. soybeans is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [190.7, 204.6].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
soybeans is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
